title: FollowUp
description: A tracked task or milestone related to a patient case. Follow-ups have a type (appointment, deadline, funding_status, treatment_progress), a due date, and a status. Overdue follow-ups are automatically detected by comparing due_date to the current date. The patient_id is denormalized from the case for efficient cross-case querying.

caseId.description: Foreign key to the Case this follow-up belongs to.

patientId.description: Denormalized patient ID from the case. Used for efficient cross-case queries in the upcoming view.

title.description: Brief title describing the follow-up task.

description.description: Detailed description of the follow-up task.

dueDate.description: Due date for the follow-up. Date-only (no time component). When due_date < current date and status is 'pending', the follow-up is considered overdue.

status.description: Follow-up status. 'overdue' is a computed status — the database stores 'pending' but the API returns 'overdue' when due_date has passed. Transitions: pending → completed, pending → cancelled.

x-computed-overdue.description: Status 'overdue' is computed at query time: status='pending' AND due_date < CURRENT_DATE → return 'overdue'. Database never stores 'overdue'.

completedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the follow-up was marked completed.

createdBy.description: ID of the User who created this follow-up.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of last modification.

examples.title: Consultation with oncologist
examples.description: First appointment at Apollo Cancer Centre. Bring biopsy report and insurance documents.
